mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 0.5% per annum, signaling accommodative monetary policy that reduces equity discount rates and supports forward equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns when FEDFUNDS stays in the ultra-low regime.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a 2-month window, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling a contraction in residential investment demand that cascades into reduced construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1189.0 thousand units as of 2022-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following a > 10% HOUST decline, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline simultaneously over a 1-month period, indicating synchronized supply-side and labor-market weakness that signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment remains below 5.0%, indicating robust consumer demand and tight labor markets that support corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 457727.0 million dollars as of 2022-01-15
regime_hint: strong demand with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both RSAFS > 0.5% MoM and UNRATE < 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 8.0% year-over-year, triggering expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL YoY > 8.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 net new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling a loss of labor market momentum that precedes broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100,000 jobs
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth < 100,000, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,200 thousand units, indicating sustained economic expansion with robust residential investment that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.0% AND HOUST > 1200 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16575.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2022-01-15
fact:HOUST at 1189.0 thousand units as of 2022-01-15
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with housing strength
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth > 2.0% and HOUST > 1200, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
